recentDecisions.text: --- If you want, next I can make the pad use the **exact shared TabBar components** (same rendering code path) so visuals + behavior are literally 1:1 instead…
### Fixed now I changed split-mode group creation to use the app’s modal prompt (`useModal().prompt`) instead of `window.prompt`.
If you want, after you test it I can keep going with the visual polish for grouping, like a fuller grouped tab strip.

lastSourceText:
yes do that too
Done. I added both: ### 1) Nix flake support Created: - `flake.nix` - `flake.lock` It now provides: - `nix develop` - `nix run .` - `nix run .#dev` - `nix run .#rebuild` - `nix run .#install-local` ### 2) Better NixOS runtime launcher Created: - `scripts/source-nix-runtime.sh` - `scripts/run-local-electron.sh` Updated: - `scripts/dev-electron-nix.sh` - `scripts/install-linux-local.sh` What changed: - the installed `ibsidian` launcher now runs through `scripts/run-local-electron.sh` - that script auto-exports common Electron runtime libs into `NIX_LD_LIBRARY_PATH` - so local launch on NixOS is more robust ### Docs updated - `README.md` - `docs/linux-install.md` ### Reinstalled and tested I reinstalled the launcher and verified it starts: ```bash ibsidian ``` also works via Nix helper: ```bash nix run "path:$PWD#install-local" ``` ## You can use it now like this From repo root: ```bash nix develop nix run .#rebuild nix run .#install-local nix run . ``` Or just: ```bash ibsidian ``` ## Notes - First `nix run` pulled a lot of dependencies and generated `flake.lock` - The Electron startup test succeeded - Existing unrelated TS issues in the repo still remain, but they do not block build/run If you want, next I can also make a **real Nix package derivation** so you can install Ibsidian with `nix profile install` instead of relying on the local source launcher.
